'''Paul Ulberg''' (born 14 November 1995) is a [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Ulberg was born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]]. He is [[Cook Islands]] descent.<ref>[https://www.cookislandsnews.com/rugby-league/internal/sport/ulberg-joins-championship-club-broncos/ Ulberg joins Championship club Broncos]</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
He came through the youth system at the [[New Zealand Warriors]], before playing for the [[Norths Devils]], [[Sunshine Coast Falcons]] and [[Wests Panthers]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>[https://leagueunlimited.com/people/show/paul-ulberg leagueunlimited stats]</ref>

====London Broncos====
Ulberg joined [[London Broncos|London]] ahead of the [[2022 RFL Championship|2022 season]] and was the top try-scorer for the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] in the [[2022 London Broncos season]].<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/london-broncos-sign-cook-islands-paul-ulberg/ London Broncos win race for Cook Islands star]</ref><ref>{{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/20220917100323/https://londonnewsonline.co.uk/ulberg-signs-back-up-for-another-championship-campaign-with-london-broncos/ Ulberg signs back up for another Championship campaign with London Broncos]}}</ref>

====Toulouse Olympique====
On 10 November 2023, it was reported that he had signed for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Nov 2023|title=Cook Islands international departs newly-promoted London Broncos for Championship move: 'We can do great things together'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/cook-islands-international-departs-newly-promoted-london-broncos-for-championship-move-we-can-do-great-things-together}}</ref>

===International career===
In 2019 Ulberg made his international début for the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] against [[South Africa national rugby league team|South Africa]].<ref>[https://medium.com/@JasonNRL/rlwc-qualifiers-south-africa-v-cook-islands-f873fcf3f1ad RLWC Qualifiers: South Africa v Cook Islands]</ref><ref>[https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/10/17/nine-players-ready-to-emerge-at-the-world-cup-9s/ Nine players ready to emerge at the World Cup 9s]</ref>

Ulberg played for the Cook Islands at the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]] and was named in the team of the tournament.<ref>[https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/international-winger-paul-ulberg-makes-championship-switch-despite-interest-from-big-clubs-45514/ International winger Paul Ulberg makes Championship switch despite interest from big clubs]</ref>

In 2022 Ulberg was named in the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup squads#Cook Islands|Cook Islands squad]] for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-squad-lists/ Full list of every squad at the Rugby League World Cup 2021]</ref>
